member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.BiDiDriver.ExecuteCommandAsync``1(WebDriverBiDi.CommandParameters)
member.summary: Asynchronously sends a command to the remote end of the WebDriver BiDi protocol and waits for the default command timeout.

member.param: The object containing settings for the command, including parameters.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.
member.exception: Thrown if an error occurs during the execution of the command.

member.param: The object containing settings for the command, including parameters.
The timeout to wait for the command to complete.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.
member.exception: Thrown if an error occurs during the execution of the command.

member.name: T:WebDriverBiDi.Browser.ClientWindowState
member.summary: The enumerated values of client window states.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Browser.ClientWindowState.Normal
member.summary: The client window state is normal.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Browser.ClientWindowState.Minimized
member.summary: The client window state is minimized, usually meaning that the window is represented by an icon, and having zero width and height.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Browser.ClientWindowState.Maximized
member.summary: The client window state is maximized, usually meaning that the window is sized to take up the full width and height of the system's current display, but retaining so-called "chrome" elements, like a menu bar, toolbar, and so on.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Browser.ClientWindowState.Fullscreen
member.summary: The client window state is full screen, usually meaning that the window's content is sized to take up the full width and height of the system's current display, and not displaying so-called "chrome" elements, like a menu bar, toolbar, and so on.

member.name: T:WebDriverBiDi.Browser.SetClientWindowStateCommandResult
member.summary: Result for getting the current client windows for the browser.setClientWindowState command.
member.remarks: This result class is essentially a copy of , as that is the type specified by the protocol. This is structured this way as commands must return a type descending from , and C# does not permit multiple inheritance. Should the structure of the info object change, this class will require updates to match.

member.summary: Creates an object derived from WebDriverBiDiEventArgs which contains information about an event.

The type of TEventArgs is not the same type as T

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

A function taking type T and returning a Task that is executed every time the event is observed.
The options to use when executing the event handler.

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a checkpoint is set for this observer.

member.summary: Gets the internal unique identifier of this observer.

member.summary: Stops observing the event.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.EventObserver`1.SetCheckpoint(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Sets a checkpoint that can be satisfied after the specified number of notifications this observer receives, capturing the objects created while waiting for the checkpoint to be fulfilled.
member.param: The number of notifications to wait for. If unspecified, defaults to 1.
member.exception: Thrown when the number of notifications specified is less than 1.
Thrown when a checkpoint is already established and not yet fulfilled.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.EventObserver`1.WaitForCheckpoint(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Waits for a checkpoint to be satisfied by having this event observer notified the number of times specified when the checkpoint was set. If the wait is successful, it means only that this observer was notified to execute the handler, not that the handler has necessarily completed execution.
member.param: A representing the timeout to wait for the checkpoint to be satisfied.
member.returns: if this observer has been notified the expected number of times before the timeout expires; otherwise, .

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.EventObserver`1.GetCheckpointTasks
member.summary: Gets the objects captured while waiting for the checkpoint to be fulfilled. Calling this method unsets the checkpoint, and transfers the ownership of the captured s to the calling method.
member.returns: An array of objects captured while waiting for the checkpoints to be fulfilled.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.EventObserver`1.UnsetCheckpoint
member.summary: Unset an established checkpoint for this observer.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.EventObserver`1.ToString
member.summary: Gets the string representation of this event observer.
member.returns: The string representation of this event observer.

member.summary: Notifies this observer that the observed event has occurred.
member.param: The object containing information about the event.
member.returns: A representing the result of the asynchronous operation.

member.name: T:WebDriverBiDi.Internal.DateTimeUtilities
member.summary: A utility class for System.DateTime values.
member.remarks: This class is intentionally marked as internal, as it only contains utility properties and methods used within this library. They should have little use for users outside of this library. Should that change, this class could be moved to another namespace and exposed for external use.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Internal.DateTimeUtilities.UnixEpoch
member.summary: Gets a DateTime representing the start of the Unix epoch (1 January 1970 12:00 AM UTC).
member.remarks: The DateTime struct introduces this property in .NET 5, but is unavailable for libraries targeting .NET Standard 2.0. This property is provided to minimize mistakes in calculating dates that are offsets from the Unix zero date.

member.summary: The handler will execute asynchronously. Order of multiple executions of the handler is not guaranteed.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the event.
The maximum number of handlers that may observe this event.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.ObservableEvent`1.EventName
member.summary: Gets the name of this observable event.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.ObservableEvent`1.MaxObserverCount
member.summary: Gets the maximum number of observers that may observe this event. A value of zero (0) indicates an unlimited number of observers.

member.summary: Gets the current number of observers that are observing this event.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.ObservableEvent`1.AddObserver(System.Action{`0},WebDriverBiDi.ObservableEventHandlerOptions,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a function to observe the event that takes an argument of type T and returns void. It will be wrapped in a Task so that it can be awaited.
member.param: An action that handles the observed event.
The options for executing the handler. Defaults to ObservableEventHandlerOptions.None, meaning the handler will attempt to execute synchronously, awaiting the result of execution.
An optional description for this observer.
member.returns: An observer for this observable event.
member.exception: Thrown when the user attempts to add more observers than this event allows.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.ObservableEvent`1.AddObserver(System.Func{`0,System.Threading.Tasks.Task},WebDriverBiDi.ObservableEventHandlerOptions,System.String)

member.param: A function returning a Task that handles the observed event.
The options for executing the handler. Defaults to ObservableEventHandlerOptions.None, meaning the handler will attempt to execute synchronously, awaiting the result of execution.
An optional description for this observer.
member.returns: An observer for this observable event.
member.exception: Thrown when the user attempts to add more observers than this event allows.

member.summary: Removes a handler for this observable event.

member.name: T:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport
member.summary: The transport object used for serializing and deserializing JSON data used in the WebDriver Bidi protocol. It uses a object to communicate with the remote end, and does no further processing of the objects serialized or deserialized. Consumers of this class are expected to handle things like awaiting the response of a WebDriver BiDi command message.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.#ctor(WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Connection)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a given command timeout and connection.
member.param: The connection used to communicate with the protocol remote end.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.OnEventReceived
member.summary: Gets an observable event that notifies when an event is received from the protocol.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.OnErrorEventReceived
member.summary: Gets an observable event that notifies when an error is received from the protocol that is not the result of a command execution.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.OnUnknownMessageReceived
member.summary: Gets an observable event that notifies when an unknown message is received from the protocol.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.OnLogMessage
member.summary: Gets an observable event that notifies when a log message is written.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.EventHandlerExceptionBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating how this should behave when an unhandled exception in a handler for a defined protocol is encountered. Defaults to ignoring exceptions, in which case, those exceptions will never be surfaced to the user.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.ProtocolErrorBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating how this should behave when a protocol error is encountered, such as invalid JSON or JSON missing required properties. Defaults to ignoring exceptions, in which case, those exceptions will never be surfaced to the user.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.UnknownMessageBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating how this should behave when an unknown message is encountered, such as valid JSON that does not match any protocol data structure. Defaults to ignoring exceptions, in which case, those exceptions will never be surfaced to the user.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.UnexpectedErrorBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating how this should behave when an unexpected error is encountered, meaning an error response received with no corresponding command. Defaults to ignoring exceptions, in which case, those exceptions will never be surfaced to the user.

member.name: P:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.Transport.LastCommandId
member.summary: Gets the ID of the last command to be added.

member.summary: Gets the connection used to communicate with the browser.

member.name: T:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.TransportErrorBehavior
member.summary: The enumerated value specifying what the should do upon encountering an error, such as a malformed JSON payload, an unexpected error response not caused by a command, or an unhandled exception in an event handler.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.TransportErrorBehavior.Ignore
member.summary: Ignore errors.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.TransportErrorBehavior.Collect
member.summary: Collect errors as they occur and throw an exception upon termination of the .

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.TransportErrorBehavior.Terminate
member.summary: Terminates the connection immediately. This will have the effect of throwing an exception upon execution of the next command.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the behavior for errors resulting from a valid JSON message that corresponds to no defined protocol command response, error response, or event definition.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the behavior for errors resulting from valid error responses that do not correspond to a command sent by the user.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the behavior for errors resulting from unhandled exceptions in user-defined event handlers for protocol events.

The exception that causes the unhandled error.

member.summary: Clears the collection of unhandled errors.

member.name: T:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.UnhandledErrorType
member.summary: The enumerated types of unhandled errors by the protocol transport.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.UnhandledErrorType.ProtocolError
member.summary: An error resulting from an unparsable protocol message, for example, invalid JSON, or JSON that does not contain required values.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.UnhandledErrorType.UnknownMessage
member.summary: An error resulting from a valid JSON protocol message, but which does not match the schema of any known command result, error, or event.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.UnhandledErrorType.UnexpectedError
member.summary: An error resulting from a valid JSON error response, but one that does not correspond to a command that the user has sent.

member.name: F:WebDriverBiDi.Protocol.UnhandledErrorType.EventHandlerException
member.summary: An error resulting from an unhandled exception in a user-defined event handler for a protocol event.
